mechanism: Anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial against Demodex, normalizes keratinization

studies.title: Azelaic acid 15% foam for papulopustular rosacea: RCT
studies.findings: Significant reduction in inflammatory lesions; FDA-approved for rosacea

notes: FDA-approved for rosacea. Well-tolerated. Also helps with associated erythema.

mechanism: Anti-inflammatory; reduces neutrophil-generated reactive oxygen species; antimicrobial

studies.title: Metronidazole for rosacea: systematic review
studies.findings: First-line topical treatment; significant reduction in papules and pustules

notes: First-line prescription treatment. Available as gel, cream, or lotion.

studies.title: Ivermectin 1% cream for rosacea: RCT
studies.findings: Superior to metronidazole 0.75% cream at 16 weeks; sustained remission

notes: FDA-approved for rosacea. Addresses Demodex directly.

studies.title: Niacinamide for barrier repair and redness: clinical study
studies.findings: Improved skin barrier and reduced erythema in sensitive skin

notes: Excellent adjunct. Very well tolerated. Helps with barrier repair.

studies.title: Traditional use; limited modern RCTs
studies.findings: Used historically for rosacea; limited modern clinical validation

notes: Often combined with sulfacetamide. Can be drying. Good for papulopustular rosacea.

studies.title: Green tea for rosacea: clinical study
studies.findings: Reduced erythema and inflammation; improved skin barrier

notes: Good for erythematotelangiectatic rosacea. Cooling effect.

mechanism: Anti-inflammatory, strengthens barrier, promotes wound healing

studies.title: Traditional use; limited rosacea-specific RCTs
studies.findings: Used for sensitive skin; limited rosacea-specific clinical trials

notes: Gentle option for sensitive, rosacea-prone skin. Adjunct therapy.

studies.title: Sunscreen for rosacea: importance of photoprotection
studies.findings: Essential for rosacea management; zinc oxide preferred for sensitive skin

notes: Essential for rosacea. Physical sunscreen preferred over chemical.

mechanism: Soothing, promotes cell proliferation, reduces irritation

studies.title: Limited rosacea-specific trials
studies.findings: Well-established soothing properties; limited rosacea-specific data

notes: Excellent for calming irritated skin. Adjunct only.

studies.title: Traditional use; limited rosacea-specific RCTs
studies.findings: Used for skin soothing; limited rosacea-specific clinical data

notes: Good for acute flare soothing. Not a primary treatment.

studies.title: Limited rosacea-specific RCTs
studies.findings: Used for redness reduction; limited rosacea-specific trials

notes: Gentle option for reducing redness. Adjunct therapy.

studies.title: Traditional use; limited rosacea-specific RCTs
studies.findings: Used for sensitive skin; limited rosacea-specific clinical data

notes: Good for sensitive skin. Can cause allergy in ragweed-sensitive individuals.

mechanism: Restores lipid barrier, reduces TEWL, protects against irritants

studies.title: Ceramide-dominant barrier repair for sensitive skin
studies.findings: Improved barrier function and reduced sensitivity

notes: Essential for rosacea-prone skin. Use gentle, ceramide-rich moisturizers.

studies.title: Panthenol for skin hydration and barrier function
studies.findings: Improved skin hydration and barrier function

notes: Excellent for barrier repair. Very well tolerated.

mechanism: Lightweight emollient; non-irritating; restores skin barrier

studies.title: Limited rosacea-specific trials; well-established emollient
studies.findings: Used as a gentle moisturizer base; limited rosacea-specific data

notes: Excellent gentle moisturizer for rosacea-prone skin.

studies.title: Limited rosacea-specific RCTs
studies.findings: Well-established soothing properties; limited rosacea-specific trials

notes: Gentle and effective. Often used in combination with other soothing agents.

mechanism: Anti-inflammatory, normalizes keratinization (may help with papulopustular subtype)

studies.title: Low-dose retinoids for rosacea: clinical study
studies.findings: Some benefit for papulopustular subtype; high risk of irritation

notes: Controversial. Can worsen erythema. Use with extreme caution.

mechanism: Balances skin microbiome, strengthens barrier, reduces inflammation

studies.title: Topical probiotics for rosacea: review
studies.findings: Promising preliminary data; more RCTs needed

notes: Emerging area. Good adjunct. Limited standardized products.

mechanism: Anti-inflammatory, soothing, restores barrier, reduces itching

studies.title: Colloidal oatmeal for sensitive skin conditions
studies.findings: Effective for soothing irritated skin; limited rosacea-specific data

notes: Excellent for acute flare soothing. FDA-approved for eczema; beneficial for rosacea.
